basics.name: Sebastian Schmon
basics.label: Leadership in AI Research & Engineering
basics.title: DPhil (Oxford)
basics.summary: I am a machine learning researcher, engineer and former professor driven by a passion for leveraging AI to tackle real-world challenges. I am proud to say that the teams under my guidance have always punched above their weight. With publications in top-tier conferences and journals, I take a collaborative approach to developing innovative algorithms and solutions that not only advance the field but also have a real world impact.

work.name: Latent Labs
bullets: Heading the development of the internal AI agent.

Supervising and implementing AI platform integration via Model Context Protocol (MCP, TypeScript) as well as other platform improvements for better integration with agents, including building an OAuth2 authentication layer.
Leading agent development and research, including Python execution engine, multi-backend support (AWS Bedrock, Google VertexAI, Anthropic), graph structure, and tool development.
Setting up and managing Kubernetes deployment and infrastructure via Terraform.

work.name: Latent Labs
bullets: Personally responsible for training the diffusion model behind the Latent-X platform, which was demonstrated to generate de-novo binders with nanomolar affinity.
Developed an improved inference algorithm (also served on the platform) that increased sample quality drastically.
Led engineering efforts to migrate cloud compute to AWS.
Developed inference pipelines for large-scale distributed sampling.
Led and organised the writing of the Latent-X paper.
Developed post-training validation scripts, including structural analysis, diversity and clustering approaches.

work.name: Altos Labs
bullets: Responsible for ideation and development of the "Perturbench" project to find and develop the most powerful models for predicting effects of perturbations.
Developed and implemented large scale AI/ML models (biological foundation models and generative models) for biological phenotypes.
Implemented and benchmarked several perturbation prediction models, including state-of-the-art architectures for single-cell data.
Involved in and supervised the whole modelling process, including foundational research, model development, engineering, MLOps, and deployment.
Novel models helped disentangle the very noisy human transcriptome and uncover mechanisms driving ageing and diseases.
Led the writing process for the Perturbench paper including the narrative.

work.name: Shift Lab
bullets: Founded by two ex-DeepMind co-founders as a bridge between academic AI research and real-world production software.
Worked with state-of-the-art generative models, including OpenAI's language models and diffusion models for image generation.
Developed ML approaches to learn representations from large language model embeddings and combine them with expert opinions on "tone of voice" for brand communications.
Built a model maximising brand communication value in terms of user engagement based on actionable attributes. Delivered prototype 3 weeks after joining.

work.name: University of Durham
bullets: Supervised PhD, MSc and BSc projects, some leading to papers accepted at CVPR and ICLR workshops.
Updated the university's "unsupervised learning" module to include deep neural network based approaches.
Organised the setup of a new doctoral training centre.
Served as industry outreach officer.

work.name: Improbable Worlds Ltd.
bullets: Leveraged new technologies for large-scale networked simulations to build generative models of complex systems and digital twins modelling e.g. Covid-19 policies, cyberattacks.
Ensured synthetic environments were a reflection of real world phenomena.
Used simulation-based inference to estimate uncertainty associated with imperfect simulations.
Built fast surrogate models (neural ODEs, normalising flows, diffusion models) reducing model complexity without losing predictive accuracy.

work.name: Foresight Works
bullets: Second employee at this technology start-up using AI to avoid large cost overruns on megaprojects and improve executive decision making.
Helped set up and brainstorm core products, including statistical forecasting techniques for project progress and cost overrun estimation.
Consulted with large international clients (>£5bn in revenue) on data science and analytics challenges.
Compiled and delivered a complete project with recommendations on financial decisions for a new £1.2bn megaproject, presented to the COO.

details: Computational statistics: Markov chain Monte Carlo, Sequential Monte Carlo, Bayesian Statistics.
Machine Learning: Variational autoencoders, representation learning.
Specialisation in Statistical Modelling and Inference, Econometrics, Monte-Carlo Methods, Survey Methodology.
Specialisation in Probability Theory, Numerical Methods and Functional Analysis. Minor in Statistics.
Specialisation in Quantitative Methods (Econometrics, Time Series, Statistics) and Economic Theory.

awards.title: Departmental teaching scholarship
awards.awarder: University of Oxford, Department of Statistics

volunteer.organization: NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, UAI, AISTATS, JMLR
